Copywriter for spiritual & wellness businesses

Copy that converts, without sounding clinical or cosmic.

Spiritual and wellness brands live or die on tone. Too clinical and your audience feels processed. Too mystical and they stop trusting you. I write homepages, sales pages, emails and app copy that hold that line, backed by ten years of conversion copywriting and wellness businesses I built myself.

What I write

The words your business runs on.

One-off projects or ongoing retainers, for practices, brands, platforms and apps. Every deliverable is written to convert, and written so AI assistants can find and recommend you. Projects start at £350; retainers priced on scope.

Acquisition

Homepages & sales pages

Your homepage, retreat page or store listing is your highest-leverage screen. I rewrite it around the transformation your client wants, not the offer you built.

Retention

Email, app & nurture copy

Welcome sequences, newsletters, in-app copy and push notifications that bring people back without nagging them. Tone-of-voice work included, so every touchpoint sounds like one brand.

Visibility

AI search optimisation

When someone asks ChatGPT or Claude for the best practitioner, brand or app in your category, do you come up? I audit and rewrite your copy so AI assistants cite you, not your competitor.

Selected work

Client proof. Founder proof.

I write for spiritual and wellness businesses as a copywriter, and I have run them as a founder. Both sides show up in the work below.

Therapy practice · Banbury, UK

Kokoro Therapy

Partial rewrite of the website for a UK body psychotherapist whose practice works across mind, body and soul at once: talk therapy, body-based work and the history a client carries with them. The challenge was capturing an approach that resists neat categories without making it sound vague. The rewrite gave each layer of her method its own clear language while keeping the whole page in one warm, grounded voice.

Delivered on brief, followed by a commissioned landing page for the practice's flagship workshop.

Charmaine McCaulay runs one of the most distinctive therapy practices in the UK. Interracial couples therapy, racism workshops, and body-based work that barely exists anywhere else. Her site had depth. What it did not have was a structure that made people stay, or the SEO to make them find her in the first place.

I audited the site, rewrote the copy to align with her clients' search intent, and restructured her offerings in the order people actually look for. Her own voice, direct, warm, unflinching, became the foundation for everything. I also wrote a founding story that had never been articulated on the site before, and embedded local SEO throughout without touching the tone.

The result: a site that finally reflects the ambition of what she has built.

"Wow, wow. This reads so beautifully and you have definitely captured my voice." — Charmaine McCaulay, Kokoro Therapy

Founder · Spiritual business

Prana & Astrology readings

Co-founded Prana, a Dutch spiritual lifestyle brand: candles, crystals, ritual sets, healing circles and women's events, with the full visual identity and every word of the copy built from the ground up. Two years after closing the platform and all its socials, I started an online astrology business, internationally oriented this time, and built it from zero entirely through written copy. A free offer generated 500+ requests in days, and the quality of the free observations converted strangers into paying clients within the first two days, with no ads, no name and no existing audience.

40+ five-star reviews in the first month. Every sale made by words alone.

Prana started as an idea between two friends with roots in India and Suriname, who grew up surrounded by spirituality and wanted to make it accessible without losing its depth. As co-founder and creative director I built the brand from the ground up: visual identity, brand positioning, content direction, product storytelling, and ongoing marketing.

The full visual identity came first. Logo, typography, colour system, and a design language that could carry across candles, crystals, ritual sets, business cards, social posts, and a webshop. Every touchpoint built to feel handmade and intentional, never mass-produced, never preachy. From there I wrote everything: the website copy, product descriptions, ritual texts that guide instead of instruct, and the brand story that ties it all together.

Alongside the product side, I built the community. Healing circles, women's empowerment events, sharing circles, breathwork sessions. Each event positioned, designed, and promoted as an extension of the brand: same tone, same warmth, same care. Online presence handled across Instagram, the webshop, Bol.com, Amazon, and Etsy, including the customer experience down to the thank you cards inside every package.

The result: a brand with a distinct voice in a crowded category, built on the conviction that spirituality should feel grounded, generous, and genuinely useful.

Body-oriented therapy · Utrecht, NL

SoulWork Company

Website copy and SEO for a Utrecht practice offering breathwork, body-oriented therapy and group embodiment work. The copy was there, but the structure was not: the offer came before trust was built, and the founder's story sat in a corner doing none of the work it could. The rewrite reordered everything around the reader.

Marjolein runs SoulWork Company from Utrecht: breathwork, body-oriented therapy, and group embodiment work for people who have done plenty of talking and are ready for something deeper. Her practice is built on years of personal work and embodied expertise, and her clients feel it the moment they meet her. The site was the part that did not quite carry that yet.

The copy was there, but the structure was not working. The offer came before trust was built. Her philosophy, the part that makes her work distinctive, got buried halfway down the page. The reader's pain was not named early enough for them to feel seen. And Marjolein's personal story, easily the strongest asset on the site, sat in a corner doing none of the work it could.

I restructured everything around her. Reordered the sections so trust builds before the offer lands. Rewrote each section from the reader's perspective. Placed Marjolein's story where it carries the most weight, framed as the reason this work exists in the first place.

"It resonates deeply. The words feel right, and so does the order." — Marjolein, SoulWork Company

Concept project · Somatic coaching · London, UK

Nadia Osei

Nadia had been practicing somatic coaching for six years. Word of mouth kept her busy. Her website did not. The site explained somatic coaching the way a textbook would, so her ideal client clicked away before feeling seen. The rewrite starts with the feeling before the method.

A site that finally meets her clients where they are.

Nadia had been practicing somatic coaching for six years. Word of mouth kept her busy. Her website did not.

The site explained somatic coaching the way a textbook would. Which means anyone landing on it without prior knowledge clicked away before the real work even started. Her ideal client has been in therapy for years, understands themselves deeply, and still feels stuck in their body. That person needs to feel seen on the homepage, not educated.

I rewrote the site around that person. Starting with the feeling before the method, building trust before making an offer, and letting Nadia's six years of practice speak for itself without over-explaining it. SEO, AEO and GEO built in throughout so she shows up where her clients are actually searching, whether that is Google or an AI tool recommending practitioners in London.

The result: a site that finally meets her clients where they are.

Concept project · Luxury retreats

Solis Retreats, Bali

Solis had stunning locations, thoughtful programming and a founder with a real story. The website read like a brochure, and people at that price point are not comparing itineraries. They decide based on how something makes them feel before they even click "enquire." I rebuilt the site around the emotional journey of the ideal client, leading with feeling before logistics and framing the founder story as the reason Solis exists.

A site that sells the transformation, not the timetable.

Solis had everything a premium retreat brand needs. Stunning locations, thoughtful programming, and a founder with a real story behind why she built it. The website told none of that.

It read like a brochure. Dates, prices, what was included. The kind of copy that describes an experience without making you feel it. The problem with that approach in luxury wellness is simple: people at that price point are not comparing itineraries. They are deciding based on how something makes them feel before they even click "enquire."

I rebuilt the site around the emotional journey of the ideal client. Someone who has achieved a lot, feels disconnected from themselves, and is looking for more than a holiday. Every page now leads with feeling before logistics. The founder story sits where it carries the most weight, framed as the reason Solis exists in the first place. By the time someone reaches the enquiry page, they are not deciding whether to book. They are deciding when.

The result: a site that sells the transformation, not the timetable.

About

Why spiritual and wellness businesses, specifically.

I grew up inside this world. I was raised in a spiritual Hindu household with Indo-Caribbean roots. As a child I visited spiritual fairs with my mother, and whenever I had a headache, she would do reiki for me. Today I still meditate and practise yoga regularly. Spirituality and wellness are essential to how I live, which is why my copy never sounds like a marketer describing your world from the outside.

The craft is ten years deep. BSc in Communication, Design & Marketing, a decade of brand messaging and conversion copy, a published children's book, and clients across the Netherlands, UK, US and Australia. British English by default, other Englishes on request.

I write for how people search now. Search is moving from Google results to AI answers. I structure copy so assistants like ChatGPT and Claude can parse, cite and recommend your business, which most copywriters are not doing yet.

Contact

Tell me where your clients drop off.

Send me your biggest drop-off point, the page, the email, the moment people leave. I will tell you what I would change, free, before you spend anything.
